publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-28 23:27:30 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Papers of the Snow family (1782-1865) of Truro, Massachusetts, include letters, land contracts, and a poem. Papers of the Gross family (1861-1884) include letters pertaining to the Civil War, a water cure, and life in Washington and Oregon.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Papers of the Snow family (1782-1865) of Truro, Massachusetts, include letters, land contracts, and a poem. Papers of the Gross family (1861-1884) include letters pertaining to the Civil War, a water cure, and life in Washington and Oregon.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Gross and Snow family papers, #882. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial PDF finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the repair of brig, amount of commission - 5% of the freights.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.
Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, Brig Silsbee, Boston, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Answer to Lombard's request for a new stove for ship; suggests another. Advises Lombard to use own judgement as to whether to take ship to Wilmington or to Texas and New Orleans.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, at the Philadelphia Packet Pier, Boston

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bradford cannot come to Boston; says it is essential that Lombard come to Plymouth - with all essential papers.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bradford wishes Lombard to give evidence as to whether certain damage to the ship could rightly be attributed to heavy gales; if so, his case for compensation is clear. wants to know what became of the logbook.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr., Plymouth to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bradford wishes Lombard to meet him in Boston and bring all papers necessary to settle up the voyage.

did.unittitle: Lemuel Bradford, Jr. to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bradford tells Lombard that the general consensus is that the latter should receive no compensation for his services at Mobile because "no prudent man would undertake to repair a vessel without first knowing how he was to pay the bills."; further criticizes Lombard's judgment is crediting certain items to the owners.

did.unittitle: Josiah Lombard, Griggsville [?] to Captain Lewis Lombard, Truro, Mass.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Josiah Lombard sends a few lines by his clerk, Andrew Wilson, who is on a buying trip in the East.
